title: Finite Ridge Regression Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite ridge-regression bad coefficient QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational ridge replay computes beta0 = 4/5 for lambda = 1, while a malformed row claims beta0 = 1, isolating a compact regularized-normal-equation conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional ridge or regularized least-squares rows only when they add distinct penalty, rank-deficiency, cross-validation, constrained-regression, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep general statistical inference and floating-point solver claims in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational design matrix, response vector, lambda value, regularized normal matrix, ridge coefficients, fitted values, residuals, RSS, penalty, and objective as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false ridge coefficient claim through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting coefficient.
Expose the pack through statistics, matrix computation, optimization, residual-bound, exact-vs-floating, and learner-query surfaces.
Keep estimator theory, regularization paths, model selection, cross-validation, rank-deficient generality, and floating-point solver behavior marked Lean or numerical-honesty horizon.
